title: Kaolin cleansing powder
subtitle: A dry, no-preservative facial cleansing powder with kaolin clay, oat flour, and lavender
excerpt: A dry cleansing powder needs no preservative because it contains no water. Mix kaolin clay, oat flour, arrowroot powder, and ground lavender, then add a small amount to wet hands to form a paste at the sink. It cleanses without over-stripping, rinses off completely, and keeps for 12 months on the shelf.
sourceNotes: Dry cleansing powder formulation: kaolin clay as primary cleansing agent; oat flour (colloidal oats) as soothing adjunct; arrowroot powder as slip agent; baking soda small amount for mild alkaline cleansing. Lavender flower powder: scent and mild antimicrobial. No water, no preservative needed. Shelf life 12 months in a sealed container.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~50 g cleansing powder (fills a 60 ml glass jar)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale all dry ingredients proportionally. Keep baking soda at 6% or below to avoid irritating sensitive skin.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Mix and store in a sealed jar. Ready immediately.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed container away from moisture. A damp spoon in the jar will cause clumping; use a dry spatula to dispense.

glossaryTerms.definition: A white cosmetic clay that absorbs excess oil and mild impurities from the skin surface. Gentler than bentonite or French green clay; suitable for sensitive and dry skin types.
A fine white starch from Maranta arundinacea root. In dry cosmetics it gives a silky, smooth feel and helps the powder blend evenly with water at the sink.

content.text: is the mildest of the cosmetic clays and forms the bulk of this cleansing powder.

gives a silky texture. Oat flour soothes. A small amount of baking soda adds mild cleansing action without pushing the pH too high for skin. Because the formula contains no water, there is no risk of microbial growth and no preservative is needed.

items.prepNote: 20%; gives smooth feel and helps disperse on wet skin
6%; mild cleansing action; reduce to 2% for sensitive skin
Optional; ground dried lavender; light scent

content.text: Weigh all ingredients into the mixing bowl. Whisk or stir until evenly combined and no lumps remain. Sieve through a fine-mesh sieve if needed. Transfer to a sealed glass jar.

Tip half a teaspoon into wet palms and work into a paste. Apply to damp skin, massage gently for 20 to 30 seconds, and rinse thoroughly with water. Use a dry spatula or spoon to dispense from the jar to keep the remaining powder dry.
12 months in a sealed container away from moisture and steam. Keep the jar lid closed between uses.
